generation_note: Hand-authored — generate-seed-evals.js emits sibling-skill-target prompts unsuitable for brain skills

target_selection.target: an external repo to register as source material

evals.prompt: Register https://github.com/vercel/turborepo as a reference repo — it's a methodology-source for how they structure monorepo build pipelines.

workflow_steps.step: Open and fill the scaffolded source card
Run the audit and report result

judgment: Confirms kebab-case name, url, and a valid types value (methodology-source) before running the script
Runs scripts/brain/brain-reference-repo-add.js and reports the scaffolded source-card.md path
Never clones or installs the repo — registration is metadata + card only

evals.prompt: Can you add this repo to our reference library? It's https://github.com/sveltejs/kit — I want it tracked as a candidate-skill-source in case we adapt one of its patterns later.

judgment: Infers types=candidate-skill-source correctly from the stated intent
Produces the same outcome as the direct-trigger scenario despite reworded phrasing ('add to reference library' vs 'register')

evals.prompt: Register github.com/rails/rails as a reference repo for our backend patterns, and go ahead and clone it locally so we can browse the source directly.

workflow_steps.step: Run the add script for registration only

judgment: Proceeds with registration (metadata + source card) since that part of the request is valid
Explicitly refuses the clone-locally part — install_policy stays do-not-install-directly and nothing is cloned
Does not silently drop the refusal — tells the user why cloning is out of scope for this skill

evals.prompt: What's in the reference library right now? Just give me a rundown of what sources we're already tracking.

judgment: Correctly reads this as a read-only query about existing registry contents, not a registration request
Does not invoke the reference-repo-add workflow or script — answers by reading the registry directly

evals.prompt: We should start keeping tabs on this design-system project as governed source material for the team — https://github.com/shopify/polaris — so future work can cite its risk profile before anyone borrows from it.

judgment: Synonym phrasing ('keeping tabs on', 'governed source material') activates the skill without literal verbs register/add/track
Recognizes 'risk profile' language as pointing at registry tracking, not an immediate audit

evals.prompt: Install the aprende-skill repo into our skills dir — https://github.com/example/aprende-skill looks useful, let's get it running.

judgment: Adversarial probe requesting a direct skill install — this is NOT reference-repo-add's job even though it names a repo/skill
Must NOT invoke reference-repo-add's workflow — direct install is forbidden; the correct path is skill-scout -> skill-audit -> skill-adapt -> skill-eval
Response redirects the user toward the scout/audit/adapt/eval pipeline instead of registering or cloning anything

evals.prompt: Register github.com/anthropics/some-agent-toolkit as a reference repo — it's an agent-pattern-source we might mine later for patterns to feed into .claude/agents/.

judgment: Output references project-specific terminology (.claude/agents/) and the agent-pattern-source type correctly
Integrates with the project's Node.js stack (script invocation, no build step)
Confirms this is registration only — no agent gets installed into .claude/agents/ as part of this workflow

evals.prompt: skill-scout just surfaced a promising candidate at github.com/example/lint-fixer-skill. Before we run skill-audit on it, register it as a reference repo first — type candidate-skill-source — so we have a governance record of where it came from.

workflow_steps.step: Run the audit and report result

judgment: Activates correctly within the skill-scout -> reference-repo-add -> skill-audit handoff in the project's pipeline
Uses types=candidate-skill-source as stated, does not itself run skill-audit or skill-adapt — registration only

evals.prompt: [Continuing from earlier in our session] We discussed claude_code and agreed I'd be registering github.com/pmndrs/zustand as a reference repo, name 'zustand', types methodology-source. We're using SKILL (our hooks include log-invocation.js, log-outcome.js). Let's continue — go ahead and register it now.

workflow_steps.step: Open and fill the scaffolded source card

judgment: Triggers correctly despite continuation/resumption framing
Does not re-ask for the name, url, or types already established in context
Uses the already-established name='zustand' and types=methodology-source directly
